Ayahs

سَبَّحَ لِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۱)
Whatever there is in the heavens and [whatever there is on] the earth glorifies Allah and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise. (1)
لَهُۥ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ يُحْىِۦ وَيُمِيتُ ۖ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۲)
To Him belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth: He gives life and brings death, and He has power over all things. (2)
هُوَ ٱلْأَوَّلُ وَٱلْـَٔاخِرُ وَٱلظَّـٰهِرُ وَٱلْبَاطِنُ ۖ وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌ (۳)
He is the First and the Last, the Manifest and the Hidden, and He has knowledge of all things. (3)

Key Points

The last Ayah of the previous Surah (Al-Waqi'ah) was about the glorification (Tasbih) of Allah: «فسبّح باسم ربّك العظیم» (So glorify the Name of your Lord, the Most Great), and the first Ayah of this Surah is also the glorification of Allah. «سبّح للّه ما فى السّموات و الارض» (Whatever is in the heavens and the earth glorifies Allah). Eight Surahs of the Qur'an begin with the glorification of Allah: Al-Isra', An-Nahl, Al-Hadid, Al-Hashr, As-Saff, Al-Jumu'ah, At-Taghabun, and Al-A'la. This word (Tasbih) appears more than ninety times in the Qur'an, while only four Surahs begin with the praise (Hamd) of Allah: Al-Hamd, Al-An'am, Saba', and Fatir. And this word (Hamd) with its derivatives appears about seventy times in the Qur'an. Perhaps the secret behind the predominance of glorification (Tasbih) over praise (Hamd) of Allah is the importance of combating polytheism (Shirk) and superstitions, their prevalence in human beliefs, and the precedence of purification over adornment. Just as in the remembrance (Adhkar) of the prayer (Salat), «سبحان اللّه» precedes «الحمدللّه».

In the divine worldview, the entire existence is glorifying Allah, even if we do not understand it: «و ان من شى ء الاّ یسبّح بحمده ولكن لاتفقهون تسبیحهم»(2) (And there is not a thing but glorifies Him with praise, but you do not understand their glorification). And this glorification is conscious. «كلٌّ قد عَلِم صلاته و تسبیحه»(3) (Each one knows its prayer and its glorification). The Qur'an has specifically mentioned the names of some of those who glorify: A) Angels. «نحن نسبّح بحمدك»(4) (We glorify [You] with Your praise). B) Heavenly thunder. «یسبّح الرّعد بحمده»(5) (The thunder glorifies [Him] with His praise). C) Birds. «الم تر انّ اللّه یسبّح له مَن فى السّموات و الارض و الطّیر»(6) (Do you not see that Allah is glorified by whoever is in the heavens and the earth, and the birds). D) Mountains. «سخّرنا مع داود الجبال یسبّحن»(7) (We subjected the mountains to David, glorifying [Him]).

The glorification of Allah is a means of salvation from distress. «فلولا انّه كان من المسبّحین . للبث فى بطنه الى یوم یبعثون»(8) (Had he not been of those who glorify [Allah], He would have remained inside its belly until the Day they are resurrected). If Yunus (Jonah) had not been among those who glorify, he would have remained in the belly of the fish until the Day of Resurrection.

The meaning of Allah being the First (Al-Awwal) and the Last (Al-Akhir) is His being eternal and everlasting. That is, He existed before everything and will exist after everything. And His existence is more manifest and apparent than any existence, yet at the same time, He is more hidden than any hidden thing. He is the Manifest (Az-Zahir) because many proofs indicate His existence, and He is the Hidden (Al-Batin) because He is aware of all the affairs of the universe.

2) Al-Isra', 44. 3) An-Nur, 41. 4) Al-Baqarah, 30. 5) Ar-Ra'd, 13. 6) An-Nur, 41. 7) Al-Anbiya', 79. 8) As-Saffat, 143 and 144.

Messages

1- Allah is free from every flaw, defect, and inability, and all existence bears witness to this fact. «سبّح للّه ما فى السّموات و الارض»

2- Those who glorify in the heavens precede those on the earth. «سبّح للّه ما فى السّموات و الارض»

3- Power is valuable only when its exercise is wise. «هو العزیز الحكیم»

4- The true Sovereign of the heavens and the earth is Allah. Human governance on earth is a conventional and metaphorical matter; let us not be deceived by it. «له ملك السّموات والارض»

5- The God Who possesses might, wisdom, sovereignty, and power, and in Whose hand is the life and death of creatures, is worthy of glorification and sanctification. «العزیز الحكیم، له ملك، قدیر یحیى و یمیت»

6- The beginning and end, the unseen and the manifest of existence, are in the hand of Allah. «هو الاول و الاخر و الظاهر و الباطن...»

7- More important than wise creation and mighty ownership is Allah's constant knowledge and supervision. «و هو بكلّ شى ء علیم» (considering the two preceding Ayahs).

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Hadid

This Surah has twenty-nine Ayahs and was revealed in Medina. The Ayahs of this Surah, besides strengthening the foundations of belief, address instructions concerning social and governmental matters, and in the first Ayahs, about twenty Divine Attributes are presented. The greatness of the Qur'an, the condition of the believers and the hypocrites on the Day of Resurrection, the destiny of previous nations, spending in the way of Allah for the achievement of social justice, and criticism of monasticism (Rahbaniyyah) and social isolation are among the topics addressed by this Surah.

Imam Sajjad عليه السلام said: Allah knew that a group of profound thinkers would come in the Last Age (Akhir az-Zaman), so He revealed Surah At-Tawhid and Ayahs from Surah Al-Hadid (so that people might know God better), and whoever attributes qualities other than these to God will perish.(1)

1) Tafsir Nur Ath-Thaqalayn.

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ فِى سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ثُمَّ ٱسْتَوَىٰ عَلَى ٱلْعَرْشِ ۚ يَعْلَمُ مَا يَلِجُ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا يَخْرُجُ مِنْهَا وَمَا يَنزِلُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَمَا يَعْرُجُ فِيهَا ۖ وَهُوَ مَعَكُمْ أَيْنَ مَا كُنتُمْ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ (٤)
It is He who created the heavens and the earth in six days; then settled on the Throne. He knows whatever enters the earth and whatever emerges from it and whatever descends from the sky and whatever ascends to it, and He is with you wherever you may be, and Allah watches what you do. (4)

Key Points

Whatever enters the earth, such as plant seeds, raindrops, tree roots, the bodies of the dead, treasures, and the like; and whatever exits the earth, such as the stems of plants and trees, water from wells and springs, various oil and mineral substances, and the like—all are manifest and known to Allah. Belief in the knowledge and presence of Allah is the best and strongest means of moral training. If a person knows that Allah is with him at every moment and that all his actions are under His supervision, he would never allow himself such extensive transgression and law-breaking.

Messages

  1. Although His power is infinite and He could create everything in an instant, His wisdom necessitates that creation be gradual. «خلق... فى ستّة ایّام»
  2. Allah has complete sovereignty over all existence and His creations. «خلق... ثمّ استوى على العرش»
  3. Allah has knowledge of all the details of existence. «یعلم ما یلج فى الارض و ما یخرج منها و ما ینزل... و ما یعرج...»
  4. We are all in the presence of Allah. «و هو معكم»
  5. Allah's knowledge is immediate (presential). «هو معكم»
  6. Allah is aware of all the details of our work. «و اللّه بما تعملون بصیر»

Ayahs

لَّهُۥ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ وَإِلَى ٱللَّهِ تُرْجَعُ ٱلْأُمُورُ (٥)
To Him belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and to Allah all matters are returned. (5)
يُولِجُ ٱلَّيْلَ فِى ٱلنَّهَارِ وَيُولِجُ ٱلنَّهَارَ فِى ٱلَّيْلِ ۚ وَهُوَ عَلِيمٌۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ (٦)
He makes the night pass into the day and makes the day pass into the night, and He knows best what is in the breasts. (6)

Key Points

The goal of the Quran is to introduce the attributes of Allah and invite people to Tawhid (monotheism) and the worship of the One God. This goal is prominent in the opening verses of the blessed Surah Al-Hadid. «و هو العزیز الحكیم، و هو على كلّ شى ءٍ قدیر، هو الّذى خلق السّموات... هو الاوّل و الآخر و هو بكلّ شى ءٍ علیم، و هو معكم، هو علیم بذات الصّدور»

The principles of governance and management can be summarized in several points: A) Knowledge and awareness of the manifest and the hidden: «یعلم ما یلج فى الارض...، و هو علیم بذات الصّدور» B) Power and capability: «و هو على كلّ شى ءٍ قدیر» C) Might and wisdom: «و هو العزیز الحكیم» D) Comprehensive supervision and presence: «و هو معكم اینما كنتم» E) Review and accountability of performance: «و الى اللّه ترجع الامور»

Messages

1- Allah is both the Creator of existence and the Manager and Planner of the affairs of existence. «خلق السّموات و الارض... له ملك السّموات و الارض»

2- Allah neither has a partner in creation, «هو الّذى خلق...», nor does He need a partner in administration. «له ملك السّموات»

3- Allah is the source of existence, «هو الاول», and the end of existence returns to Him. «و الى اللّه ترجع الامور»

4- Existence has a purpose and is moving towards Allah. «الى اللّه ترجع الامور»

5- The rotation of night and day and the lengthening and shortening of the days are examples of Divine planning. «له ملك السّموات... یولج الّیل...»

6- Allah is aware of our deeds, «بما تعملون بصیر», and also of our thoughts and intentions. «علیم بذات الصّدور»

Ayah

ءَامِنُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ وَأَنفِقُوا۟ مِمَّا جَعَلَكُم مُّسْتَخْلَفِينَ فِيهِ ۖ فَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مِنكُمْ وَأَنفَقُوا۟ لَهُمْ أَجْرٌ كَبِيرٌ (۷)
Have faith in Allah and His Apostle, and spend out of that of which He has made you heirs. There is a great reward for those of you who have faith and spend [in Allah’s way]. (7)

Key Points

This verse addresses the believers, and the intent of the command to believe, "آمنوا بالله و رسوله" (Believe in Allah and His Messenger), is to act upon the requirements of faith. This is evident as the verse subsequently states: You who claim faith must spend (in charity/infaq) in the way of Allah to prove your claim. (9) The meaning of man being a successor (Khalifah) in the phrase "جعلكم مستخلفین فیه" (He made you successors therein) is either that Allah has appointed man as His vicegerent on Earth, as indicated by the verse "انّى جاعل فى الارض خلیفة" (10) (Indeed, I am going to place upon the earth a successor). In this case, the meaning of the verse becomes: O believers! You are the successors of Allah on Earth, and what you possess is His property held by you. Therefore, spend (infaq) on others from what Allah has bestowed upon you. The other meaning is that you, the people of today, are the successors of those who came before you, and the wealth in your hands was previously in theirs. Therefore, spend from this wealth, for one day it will no longer be yours, and this wealth will be in the hands of your heirs. (11)

9) Tafsir al-Mizan. 10) Al-Baqarah, 30. 11) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Faith is valuable when accompanied by helping the poor. "آمِنوا... أنفِقوا" (Believe... Spend).

2- Spending (Infaq) is valuable when it stems from faith and a divine motivation. "آمِنوا... أنفِقوا" (Believe... Spend).

3- Everything man possesses is a loan and a trust; the true Owner is only Allah. "جعلكم مستخلفین فیه" (He made you successors therein).

4- Although faith and spending (infaq) are duties and obligations, Allah has set a reward for them. "فالّذین آمنوا... و أنفقوا لهم أجر كبیر" (So those who have believed... and spent, for them is a great reward). Yes, reward is the key to encouraging action.

Ayah

وَمَا لَكُمْ لَا تُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ ۙ وَٱلرَّسُولُ يَدْعُوكُمْ لِتُؤْمِنُوا۟ بِرَبِّكُمْ وَقَدْ أَخَذَ مِيثَـٰقَكُمْ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۸)
Why should you not have faith in Allah when the Apostle invites you to have faith in your Lord, and He has certainly made a covenant with you, if you are [genuinely] faithful? (8)

Key Points

The phrase "ان كنتم مؤمنین" (if you are believers) at the end of the Ayah indicates that the address of the Ayah is directed at the believers. Therefore, the intent of "ما لكم لاتؤمنون باللّه" (What is wrong with you that you do not believe in Allah?) is acting upon the requirements of faith. Meaning, you who possess faith, why do you do something as if you do not believe in Allah? Although some commentators have stated that the intent of the covenant (Mithaq) is the World of Dhar (pre-existence), the covenant of the World of Dhar was taken from all people, whereas the covenant in this Ayah is a specific covenant taken from the believers. "أخذ میثاقكم ان كنتم مؤمنین"

Messages

1- In propagation (Da'wah), sometimes reproach is necessary. "و ما لكم لاتؤمنون"

2- Allah completes the proof (Hujjah) against the people. "و الرّسول یدعوكم"

3- The Prophets invite people toward Allah, contrary to others who invite people toward themselves. "لتؤمنوا بربّكم"

4- Allah invites people to believe in the Messenger, "آمنوا باللّه و رسوله" (Believe in Allah and His Messenger), and the Messenger also asks people to believe in Allah. "یدعوكم لتؤمنوا بربّكم" (He calls you to believe in your Lord).

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِى يُنَزِّلُ عَلَىٰ عَبْدِهِۦٓ ءَايَـٰتٍۭ بَيِّنَـٰتٍ لِّيُخْرِجَكُم مِّنَ ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتِ إِلَى ٱلنُّورِ ۚ وَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِكُمْ لَرَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۹)
It is He who sends down manifest signs to His servant that He may bring you out of darkness into light, and indeed Allah is most kind and merciful to you. (9)

Key Points

All divine programs are based on wisdom and reason, although only a fraction of them has been explained to us, including: 1. The reason for prophethood is the salvation of people. «لیخرجكم من الظّلمات الى النّور» 2. The reason for prayer (Salat) is His remembrance and gratitude towards Him. «اقم الصلاة لذكرى»(12) 3. The reason for fasting (Sawm) is piety (Taqwa). «كتب علیكم الصیام... لعلّكم تتقون»(13) 4. The reason for Hajj is presence and participation in the benefits and rituals of Hajj. «لیشهدوا منافع لهم»(14) 5. The reason for Jihad is the removal of discord (Fitnah) and the preservation of the faith/school of thought (Maktab). «حتى لاتكون فتنة»(15) 6. The reason for retribution (Qisas) is a dignified life. «و لكم فى القصاص حیاة»(16) 7. The reason for Hijab is the purification and cleansing of hearts. «ذلكم أطهر لقلوبكم و قلوبهنّ»(17) 8. The reason for Zakat is purity. «خذ من أموالهم صدقة تطهّرهم»(18) 9. The reason for prohibiting wine and gambling is being kept away from the path of Allah. «یصدّكم عن ذكر اللّه و عن الصّلاة»(19)

The salvation of humanity from the darknesses of ignorance, oppression, polytheism (Shirk), superstition, and division is dependent solely on turning towards the light of revelation. «كتاب أنزلناه الیك لتخرج النّاس من الظّلمات الى النّور»(20)

The word Inna (انّ), the letter Lam (ل), the nominal sentence structure (Jumla Ismiyya), and the intensive form (Sighah Mubalaghah) in the phrase «انّ اللّه بكم لرؤف رحیم» indicate that divine compassion (Ra’fah) and mercy (Rahmah) are permanent, profound, extensive, and certain.

12) Taha, 14. 13) Baqarah, 183. 14) Hajj, 28. 15) Baqarah, 193. 16) Baqarah, 179. 17) Ahzab, 53. 18) Tawbah, 103. 19) Ma’idah, 91. 20) Ibrahim, 1.

Messages

1- Sincere servitude paves the way for the descent of divine favors. «ینزّل على عبده»

2- The preacher (Muballigh) must be equipped with clear argumentation. «و الرسول یدعوكم... آیاتٍ بینات»

3- The main goal of the prophets is to save people from the darknesses of ignorance, polytheism (Shirk), and division. «لیخرجكم من الظّلمات الى النّور»

4- The clear path is the only path of Allah; anything other than it is darkness. «الظّلمات... النّور» (The word al-Zulumat (الظّلمات) is used in the plural form, but the word al-Nur (النّور) is used in the singular form throughout the entire Quran.)

5- The descent of revelation is based on the principle of grace (Qā'idah Luṭf). «هوالّذى ینزّل... انّ اللّه بكم لرؤف رحیم»

6- The best sign of divine compassion (Ra’fah) and mercy (Rahmah) is the guidance of His servants. «لیخرجكم من الظّلمات الى النّور انّ اللّه بكم لرؤف رحیم»

Ayah

وَمَا لَكُمْ أَلَّا تُنفِقُوا۟ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ وَلِلَّهِ مِيرَٰثُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ لَا يَسْتَوِى مِنكُم مَّنْ أَنفَقَ مِن قَبْلِ ٱلْفَتْحِ وَقَـٰتَلَ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَعْظَمُ دَرَجَةً مِّنَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَنفَقُوا۟ مِنۢ بَعْدُ وَقَـٰتَلُوا۟ ۚ وَكُلًّا وَعَدَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْحُسْنَىٰ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ (۱٠)
Why should you not spend in the way of Allah, when to Allah belongs the heritage of the heavens and the earth? Those of you who spent [their means] and fought before the victory are not equal [to others]. They are greater in rank than those who have spent and fought afterwards. Yet Allah has promised the best reward to each and Allah is well aware of what you do. (10)

Key Points

In the Noble Qur'an, hundreds of verses discuss helping the deprived, presented in the form of Zakat, Khums, Sadaqah (charity), Infaq (spending), Qardh al-Hasanah (goodly loan), feeding, self-sacrifice (Ithar), and so on. Likewise, [it] contains instructions regarding the type and amount of spending, and the conditions of the spender and the recipient. In the previous verses of this Surah, this verse, and the subsequent verses, special attention has been given to the issue of helping the deprived. In the seventh verse, He commanded spending (Infaq) and said: «انفقوا مما جعلكم مستخلفین فیه» (Spend out of that in which He has made you successors). You are the inheritors of those who came before; spend from what you have inherited. In this verse, He reprimands those who abandon spending, «و ما لكم الاّ تنفقوا» (And what is wrong with you that you do not spend?), and warns that what you have acquired, you will leave behind as inheritance and depart. Why do you not spend with your own hands? «و للّه میراث السّموات و الارض» (And to Allah belongs the inheritance of the heavens and the earth). Precedence and priority in good deeds is one of the criteria for evaluation. Spending under difficult conditions before the Conquest of Mecca is not the same as spending under normal conditions after it.

Messages

  1. Just as disbelief incurs reprimand, «و ما لكم لاتؤمنون» (And what is wrong with you that you do not believe?), abandoning spending (Infaq) also incurs reprimand. «ما لكم الاّ تنفقوا»
  2. Sincerity (Ikhlas) is a condition for the acceptance of deeds. «فى سبیل اللّه» (in the way of Allah).
  3. Comparison is one of the useful educational and pedagogical methods. «لایستوى منكم» (Not equal among you).
  4. Sacrificing both wealth and life in the way of Allah is necessary. «انفق... و قاتل» (Spend... and fight).
  5. Spending (Infaq) is the support for fighters on the front lines of Jihad. «انفق... و قاتل»
  6. Mujahidin (those who strive) who have sacrificed their lives and wealth must hold the highest status in the Islamic society. «اولئك اعظم درجة» (Those are greater in degree).
  7. Those who join later should not be neglected or ignored because of the precedence of the former ones. «كلاّ وعد اللّه الحسنى» (To all, Allah has promised the best).
  8. Sometimes the form of the action may be the same, but Allah is aware of the motives and conditions of the individuals. «و اللّه بما تعملون خبیر» (And Allah is All-Aware of what you do).

Ayah

مَّن ذَا ٱلَّذِى يُقْرِضُ ٱللَّهَ قَرْضًا حَسَنًا فَيُضَـٰعِفَهُۥ لَهُۥ وَلَهُۥٓ أَجْرٌ كَرِيمٌ (۱۱)
Who is it that will lend Allah a good loan, that He may multiply it for him and [that] there may be a noble reward for him? (11)

Key Points

"Qardh" (loan/lending) means cutting off and separating a portion of one's wealth. "Miqradh" is the cutting tool, and "Quradhah" refers to wealth that has been separated from the main body. A loan to Allah is a form of spending (infaq) that will be repaid manifold in the Hereafter. The loan (qardh) in the Quran is accompanied by the word "Hasan" (good), meaning it should be paid immediately, without obligation (minnah), before the beggar asks, in abundance, and for useful and necessary purposes.

Qardh al-Hasanah (the good loan) has blessings: reducing resentment and increasing affection, preserving people's dignity and preventing their bankruptcy, reforming the economic structure of society, preventing the spread of usury (riba), controlling greed and stinginess, moderating wealth, and fostering a culture of cooperation.

Allah, to Whom belongs the inheritance of the heavens and the earth, and Who stated twice in the preceding verses: «له ملك السّموات و الارض» (To Him belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth), asks people for a loan in this verse to encourage them and emphasize the importance of Qardh al-Hasanah. «من ذا الّذى یقرض اللّه...» (Who is it that would loan Allah...).

Islam opposes every type of stagnation and weakness. 1. Stagnation of nature. «من وجد ماء و ترابا ثم افتقر فابعده اللّه»(21) Whoever possesses water and land but does not strive and remains poor, is far removed from Divine Mercy. 2. Stagnation of human energy. «اللهمّ انّى اعوذ بك من الكسل»(22) O God! I seek refuge in You from laziness. 3. Stagnation of wealth. «الّذین یكنزون الذهب و الفضّة و لم ینفقونها...»(23) 4. Scientific, moral, and spiritual stagnation. «زدنى علما»(24), «زادهم هدى»(25), «من استوى یوماه فهو مغبون»(26) Whoever has two days equal to each other is a loser. 5. Stagnation of thought. «من استبدّ برأیه هلك»(27) Not seeking consultation, being autocratic in one's opinion, and acting alone leads to destruction.

21) Wasā’il, Vol. 17, p. 40. 22) Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 585. 23) Tawbah, 34. 24) Tā Hā, 114. 25) Muhammad, 17. 26) Bihār, Vol. 68, p. 173. 27) Wasā’il, Vol. 12, p. 40.

Messages

1- Allah has placed Himself on the side of the deprived and asks people for a loan. It is as if Allah has become their guarantor and has guaranteed the reward for the loan. «من ذا الّذى یقرض اللّه»

2- A good deed has greater value if it becomes a habit and disposition. «یقرض اللّه...»

3- The act itself is not important; its goodness (Hasan) is important. «قرضاً حسناً»

4- Lending is not a reduction of wealth; it is an increase thereof. «فیضاعفه»

5- Where the task is difficult, a strong incentive is required. (Since detaching oneself from wealth is hard, Allah proposes a manifold reward.) «فیضاعفه له و له أجر كریم»

6- Lending is a sign of generosity (karamah), and Allah has designated a generous reward (Ajr Karim) for the generous person. «و له أجر كریم»

7- To educate and foster the growth of others, they must be honored. «و له أجر كریم»

Ayah

يَوْمَ تَرَى ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ يَسْعَىٰ نُورُهُم بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَبِأَيْمَـٰنِهِم بُشْرَىٰكُمُ ٱلْيَوْمَ جَنَّـٰتٌ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ (۱۲)
The day you will see the faithful, men and women, with their light moving swiftly in front of them and on their right, [and they are greeted with the words:] ‘There is good news for you today! Gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them [forever]. That is the great success.’ (12)

Key Points

We read in Ayah 9 that the prophets invite people from darknesses to light. This Ayah states: Those who found their way in this world from the darknesses of ignorance to the light of knowledge, from polytheism to monotheism, and from dispersion to unity, that light will move ahead of them and guide them in the Hereafter. We also read in Ayah 157 of Surah Al-A'raf: «و اتبعوا النّور الّذى أنزل معه» (And follow the Light which was sent down with him) — follow the luminous commands of the Quran that accompany the Prophet. On the Day of Judgment, the believers will possess a light, which is referred to in this Ayah, the next Ayah, and Ayah 8 of Surah At-Tahrim. «... النبىّ و الّذین آمنوا معه نورهم یسعى بین أیدیهم و بایمانهم یقولون ربّنا أتمم لنا نورنا...» The light of the Prophet and the believers moves ahead of them, and they ask Allah to perfect their light.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, people will see one another and become aware of the condition of others. «یوم ترى...»

2- Men and women are equal in receiving divine reward and spiritual perfection. «المؤمنین والمؤمنات»

3- The light of faith will be embodied on the Day of Judgment and will guide the believers toward Paradise. «یسعى نورهم بین أیدیهم و بایمانهم»

4- The glad tidings of a blessing are themselves a spiritual and psychological blessing. «بشراكم الیوم»

5- True salvation manifests itself on the Day of Judgment. «خالدین فیها ذلك هو الفوز العظیم»

Ayah

يَوْمَ يَقُولُ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقُونَ وَٱلْمُنَـٰفِقَـٰتُ لِلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱنظُرُونَا نَقْتَبِسْ مِن نُّورِكُمْ قِيلَ ٱرْجِعُوا۟ وَرَآءَكُمْ فَٱلْتَمِسُوا۟ نُورًا فَضُرِبَ بَيْنَهُم بِسُورٍ لَّهُۥ بَابٌۢ بَاطِنُهُۥ فِيهِ ٱلرَّحْمَةُ وَظَـٰهِرُهُۥ مِن قِبَلِهِ ٱلْعَذَابُ (۱۳)
The day the hypocrites, men and women, will say to the faithful, ‘Please wait, that we may glean something from your light!’ They will be told: ‘Go back and grope for light!’ Then there will be set up between them a wall with a gate, with mercy within and punishment without. (13)

Key Points

«انظرونا» is derived from the root «نظر» (nazara). If this word appears without «اِلى» (ila), it means waiting or granting respite, similar to this Ayah and the Ayah «لایخفّف عنهم العذاب و لا هم ینظرون» (28). And if it appears with «اِلى», it means looking, like the Ayah «ینظرون الیك نظر المغشىّ علیه من الموت» (29).

«اقتباس» (Iqtibas) means taking a piece of fire.

«سُور» (Sur) means a high wall, tower, or rampart that was built around cities in ancient times to prevent the entry of strangers.

On the Day of Judgment, the criminals seek help from four groups:

A) From their leaders. «فقال الضعفاء للذین استكبروا انّا كنّا لكم تبعاً فهل أنتم مغنون عنّا من عذاب اللّه...» (30) (Can you save us from the punishment of Allah?), but they receive a negative reply.

B) From the believers, as we read in the Ayah under discussion. «انظرونا نقتبس من نوركم»

C) From the angels appointed over Hell. «قال الّذین فى النّار لخزنة جهنم ادعوا ربّكم یخفّف عنّا یوماً من العذاب» (31) (Ask your Lord to lighten the punishment for us for one day), but again the reply is negative.

D) They blame Satan, saying that he led them astray and their sin is upon him. Satan says: «فلاتلومونى و لوموا أنفسكم» (32) (Do not blame me, but blame yourselves).

28) Al-Baqarah, 162. 29) Muhammad, 20. 30) Ibrahim, 21. 31) Ghafir, 49. 32) Ibrahim, 22.

Messages

1- The disease of hypocrisy knows no gender. «المنافقون و المنافقات»

2- Those who refused in this world to emerge from the darkness of ignorance, polytheism, and division will be in darkness on the Day of Judgment and will beg others for light. «انظرونا نقتبس من نوركم»

3- The humiliation and disgrace of the hypocrites on the Day of Judgment is both verbal, «قیل ارجعوا ورائكم» (It was said: Go back behind you), and practical. «فضرب بینهم بسورٍ» (Then a wall was erected between them).

4- The hypocrite in this world is outwardly with the believers but inwardly with the disbelievers. On the Day of Judgment, too, he comes with the believers up to the gate of Paradise, but enters Hell along with the disbelievers. «فضرب بینهم بسور... باطنه فیه الرحمة و ظاهره من قبله العذاب»

Ayah

يُنَادُونَهُمْ أَلَمْ نَكُن مَّعَكُمْ ۖ قَالُوا۟ بَلَىٰ وَلَـٰكِنَّكُمْ فَتَنتُمْ أَنفُسَكُمْ وَتَرَبَّصْتُمْ وَٱرْتَبْتُمْ وَغَرَّتْكُمُ ٱلْأَمَانِىُّ حَتَّىٰ جَآءَ أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ وَغَرَّكُم بِٱللَّهِ ٱلْغَرُورُ (۱٤)
They will call out to them, ‘Did we not use to be with you?’ They will say, ‘Yes! But you cast yourselves into perdition. You awaited and were skeptical, and [false] hopes deceived you until the edict of Allah came, and the Deceiver deceived you concerning Allah. (14)

Key Points

«فتنتم انفسكم» means you subjected yourselves to the trial (or temptation) of hypocrisy. «تربّص» means waiting (or anticipating). «امانىّ» is the plural of «اُمنیّه», meaning wish or desire, and «غَرور», meaning deceiver, is one of the titles of Satan. A believer does not wish ill upon anyone. «لاتجعل فى قلوبنا غلاً للذین آمنوا»(33) But the hypocrite awaits misfortune for others. «تربّصتم» A believer possesses certainty (yaqīn), but the hypocrite is drowned in doubt. «ارتبتم» A believer is realistic, while the hypocrite is fanciful. «غرّتكم الامانىّ» As soon as a believer encounters Satan and his whispers, he remembers (Allah). «اذا مسّهم طائف من الشّیطان تذكّروا»(34) But the hypocrite is deceived. «غرّكم باللّه الغَرور» Based on other verses, the hypocrites used to think that religion was the cause of the believers' deception, saying: «غرّ هؤلاء دینهم»(35) Or they would say: The promises of Allah and the Messenger are nothing but delusion, «ما وعدنا اللّه و رسوله الا غرورا»(36) But this verse replies that Satan and prolonged desires are the means of deception. «غرّتكم الامانى - غرّكم باللّه الغَرور»

33) حشر، 10. 34) اعراف، 201. 35) انفال، 49. 36) احزاب، 12.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, there will be conversations between the hypocrites and the believers. «ینادونهم» (The imperfect verb form indicates continuity/repetition.)

2- The hypocrites' conversation is from behind a wall, accompanied by cries and pleas for help. «ینادونهم»

3- On the Day of Judgment, worldly memories will not be forgotten. «أ لم نكن معكم»

4- Physical and superficial companionship is not important; intellectual and practical companionship is necessary. «ألم نكن معكم قالوا بلى »

5- The danger lies when a person deceives himself and sees ugly deeds as beautiful, even though he knows they are ugly. «فتنتم انفسكم»

6- Long and far-reaching desires for worldly life pave the way for doubt and skepticism regarding the Day of Judgment. «و ارتبتم و غرّتكم الامانىّ»

7- Satan may deceive man by promising divine forgiveness and pardon, making him arrogant regarding Allah's mercy. «غرّكم باللّه الغَرور»

Ayah

فَٱلْيَوْمَ لَا يُؤْخَذُ مِنكُمْ فِدْيَةٌ وَلَا مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ۚ مَأْوَىٰكُمُ ٱلنَّارُ ۖ هِىَ مَوْلَىٰكُمْ ۖ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (۱٥)
So today no ransom shall be taken from you or the faithless. The Fire will be your abode: it is your [ultimate] refuge and an evil destination.’ (15)

Key Points

The meaning of "مَوْلَاكُم" (your master/protector) here is helper (ناصر) and supporter (یاور). This sentence is a form of taunt directed at the hypocrites. It means that your only path to salvation and your only helper is the Fire, which will cure your pain. (37) One of the differences between this world (dunya) and the Hereafter (akhirah) is the acceptance of ransom (fidyah) in this world and its rejection in the Hereafter. On the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah), the criminal would wish to ransom himself with all his close relatives, from his children, spouse, and brother, up to all the people on earth, but it will not be accepted. «یودّ المجرم لو یفتدى من عذاب یومئذٍ ببنیه و صاحبته و أخیه و فصیلته التى تؤویه و مَن فى الارض جمیعاً ثم یُنجیه كلاّ انّها لظى» (38)

37) Tafsir al-Mizan. 38) Al-Ma'arij, 11-15.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, worldly means are ineffective. «فالیوم لایؤخذ منكم فدیة»

2- The hypocrite and the disbeliever are companions to one another in Hell. «لایؤخذ منكم... ولا من الّذین كفروا»

3- The Day of Judgment is glad tidings for the believer. «بشراكم الیوم» But for the hypocrite and the disbeliever, it is despair and hopelessness. «لایؤخذ منكم فدیة... مأواكم النّار»

4- The Master (Mawla) of the believers is Allah, «نعم المولى و نعم النّصیر» (39) but the hypocrites and disbelievers have no master except the Fire. «هى مولاكم»

39) Al-Anfal, 40.

Ayah

۞ أَلَمْ يَأْنِ لِلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَن تَخْشَعَ قُلُوبُهُمْ لِذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ وَمَا نَزَلَ مِنَ ٱلْحَقِّ وَلَا يَكُونُوا۟ كَٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ مِن قَبْلُ فَطَالَ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْأَمَدُ فَقَسَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ ۖ وَكَثِيرٌ مِّنْهُمْ فَـٰسِقُونَ (۱٦)
Is it not time yet for those who have faith that their hearts should be humbled for Allah’s remembrance and toward the truth which has come down [to them], and to be not like those who were given the Book before? Time took its toll on them and so their hearts were hardened, and many of them are transgressors. (16)

Key Points

«Yā'n» (یَأن) is derived from «ān» (آن), meaning absolute time. In this Ayah, both the cause of humility (Khushu')—which is the remembrance of Allah, «takhsha'u qulūbuhum li-dhikri Allāh»—and the cause of hardness of heart—which is prolonged distance from the Book of Allah—are mentioned. «fa-ṭāla 'alayhimu al-amadu fa-qasat qulūbuhum». We also read in narrations (Riwāyāt): The period of the occultation (Ghaybah) of Hadrat Mahdi عليه السلام will be so prolonged that groups upon groups of people will lose their religion. (40)

In some verses of the Quran, similar to the Ayah under discussion, Allah asks the believers not to be like certain individuals and groups, while in other verses, He commands them to be like specific role models. Now we cast a brief glance at these two categories of verses:

A) Be with the truthful. «kūnū ma'a aṣ-ṣādiqīn» (41) Join the righteous. «alḥiqnī bi-ṣ-ṣāliḥīn» (42), «wa akun mina aṣ-ṣāliḥīn» (43) Be with the virtuous. «tawaffanā ma'a al-abrār» (44) Be with the patient. «wa ka-ayyin min nabīyin qātala ma'ahu ribbīyūna kathīrun fa-mā wahanū li-mā aṣābahum fī sabīli Allāh wa mā ḍa'ufū wa mā astakānū» (45) How many a prophet fought, and along with him, many devoted followers (Rībīyūn) fought and were killed, yet they resisted the hardships that afflicted them in the way of Allah, and they neither weakened nor surrendered. Be with the Prophet and His true followers. «lakum fī rasūli Allāh uswatun» (46), «qad kānat lakum uswatun ḥasanatun fī Ibrāhīma wa alladhīna ma'ahu» (47) Be among those who are grateful for blessings. «kun mina ash-shākirīn» (48) Be among the people of worship and servitude. «kun mina as-sājidīn» (49) Be those who act upon the commands of the Lord and are godly. «kūnū rabbānīyīn» (50) Be among those who submit to Allah. «wa umirtu an akūna mina al-muslimīn» (51) Be with the people of faith. «wa umirtu an akūna mina al-mu'minīn» (52) Be maintainers of equity and justice. «kūnū qawwāmīna bi-l-qisṭ» (53) Be maintainers of Allah's commands. «kūnū qawwāmīna li-llāh» (54) Be helpers of Allah's religion. «kūnū anṣāra Allāh» (55)

B) Do not be hypocritical. «wa lā takūnū ka-alladhīna qālū sami'nā wa hum lā yasma'ūn» (56) And do not be like those (hypocrites) who said, "We hear," while in reality, they did not hear. Do not be ostentatious (Riyā'kār). «wa lā takūnū ka-alladhīna kharajū min diyārihim baṭaran wa ri'ā'a an-nās» (57) And do not be like those (hypocrites) who left their homes and dwellings for the purpose of ostentation and showing off to people. Do not be covenant breakers. «wa lā takūnū ka-allatī naqaḍat ghazlahā min ba'di quwwatin ankāthā» (58) And do not be like the woman who untwisted and unraveled the threads she had spun and twisted after strengthening them. Do not annoy or distress the Prophet. «lā takūnū ka-alladhīna ādhaw Mūsā» (59) And do not be like the Jews who used to harass and distress their Prophet, Hadrat Musa. Do not forget Allah. «lā takūnū ka-alladhīna nasū Allāha fa-ansāhum anfusahum» (60) Do not be like those who forgot Allah, for that leads to the punishment of self-forgetfulness. Do not despair. «fa-lā takun mina al-qāniṭīn» (61), «lā taqnaṭū min raḥmati Allāh» (62) Do not be ungrateful and disbelieving (Kāfir). «lā takūnū ka-alladhīna kafarū» (63) Do not be a helper or supporter of criminals. «fa-lan akūna ẓahīran li-l-mujrimīn» (64) Do not be doubtful or hesitant. «fa-lā takun mina al-mumtarīn» (65) Do not be hostile toward truth and reality. «wa lā takun li-l-khā'inīna khaṣīmā» (66) Do not contend or show enmity toward others on behalf of the treacherous. Do not be among the heedless (Ghāfilīn) and the ignorant. «wa lā takun mina al-ghāfilīn» (67), «wa lā takūnanna mina al-jāhilīn» (68) Do not deem the divine verses and signs false. «wa lā takūnanna mina alladhīna kadhdhabū bi-āyāti Allāh fa-takūna mina al-khāsirīn» (69) And do not be among those who denied the verses of Allah, lest you become one of the losers. Do not be a supporter of the disbelievers (Kāfirīn) and the irreligious. «fa-lā takūnanna ẓahīran li-l-kāfirīn» (70) In transactions, do not be short-changers or over-chargers. «wa lā takūnū mina al-mukhsirīn» (71) Do not have division and disagreement. «wa lā takūnū ka-alladhīna tafarraqū wa ikhtalafū» (72)
