Messages

1- Faith without humility of the heart is subject to reproach. «a-lam ya'ni li-lladhīna āmanū an takhsha'a qulūbuhum»

2- Use the leverage of faith when inviting to humility. (Since you have faith, why do you lack humility?) «a-lam ya'ni li-lladhīna āmanū an takhsha'a qulūbuhum»

3- The goal of the revelation of divine verses is the humility of hearts. «takhsha'u qulūbuhum... wa mā nazala mina al-ḥaqq»

4- The remembrance of Allah (Dhikr) and the recitation of Quranic verses prepare the ground for the heart's humility. «takhsha'u qulūbuhum li-dhikri Allāh wa mā nazala mina al-ḥaqq»

5- Remembering divine favors, and remembering His pardon, wrath, and traditions (Sunan), is a means of humility. «an takhsha'a qulūbuhum li-dhikri Allāh»

6- We must learn lessons from the history of previous generations. «wa lā yakūnū ka-alladhīna...»

7- The continuation of heedlessness leads to hardness of heart. «fa-ṭāla 'alayhimu al-amadu fa-qasat qulūbuhum»

8- Divine traditions (Sunan) are the same for all nations (Ummahs). «wa lā yakūnū ka-alladhīna... min qabl»

9- We must not misuse the divine respite. «fa-ṭāla 'alayhimu al-amadu fa-qasat qulūbuhum»

10- Hardness of heart is the ground for transgression (Fisq) and sin. «fa-qasat qulūbuhum wa kathīrun minhum fāsiqūn»

11- The means of guidance alone are not sufficient; spiritual readiness is necessary. «ūtū al-kitāb... fa-qasat qulūbuhum..»

Ayah

ٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُحْىِ ٱلْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا ۚ قَدْ بَيَّنَّا لَكُمُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ (۱۷)
Know that Allah revives the earth after its death. We have certainly made the signs clear for you, so that you may exercise your reason. (17)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the humility (khushū') of the heart through the remembrance of Allah was discussed; in this Ayah, the revival of the earth after its death is mentioned. We read in a Hadith: The meaning of the life of the earth after its death is the life of the earth through the establishment of justice by Hadrat Mahdi عليه السلام, after its death due to tyranny and oppression. (73) Today, humanity is searching for signs of life on other planets and celestial bodies, but so far, nothing has been found. However, life is flowing throughout the entire globe: in the depths of the oceans, on the surface of the earth, and in the heights of the heavens; in microscopic organisms, in various types of plants and animals, including humans. This Ayah counts the existence of life on this earthly sphere as one of the signs of Divine power and majesty, and points to it. A blessing in which many factors are effective, such as the Earth's rotation around the sun, the distance of the Earth from the sun, the existence of seas and oceans, and the descent of rain.

73) Tafsir-e Nemūneh.

Messages

1- Spiritual life is the humility of the heart, and material life is spring after autumn. «تخشع قلوبهم... یحیى الارض»

2- The educator must utilize natural phenomena in teaching. «بیّنّا لكم الآیات»

3- One must look at natural phenomena with deep insight. «اعلموا... تعقلون»

4- A tender heart and a precise intellect are necessary. The humility of the heart is mentioned alongside intellect and contemplation. «تخشع قلوبهم... تعقلون»

5- Natural phenomena are a classroom for human growth. «یحیى الارض... لعلّكم تعقلون»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلْمُصَّدِّقِينَ وَٱلْمُصَّدِّقَـٰتِ وَأَقْرَضُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ قَرْضًا حَسَنًا يُضَـٰعَفُ لَهُمْ وَلَهُمْ أَجْرٌ كَرِيمٌ (۱۸)
Indeed the charitable men and women and those who lend Allah a good loan—it shall be multiplied for them, and there will be a noble reward for them. (18)

Key Points

In Ayah 16, the heart (qalb and dil) was discussed; in Ayah 17, thought and intellect (fikr and andisheh) were discussed; and in this Ayah, human behavior and action (raftar va kerdar) were discussed. Therefore, in education and training, a step-by-step method is necessary. In Ayah 16, the discourse is presented with a question, "ألم يأن" (Has the time not come?). In the next step, the discussion is about spiritual readiness and the humility (khushu') of the hearts. In the step after that, the discussion is about knowledge and intellect ('aql), and in this Ayah, the issue is presented with decisiveness. "إنّ المصّدّقين..." (Indeed, the charitable men...). Yes, helping the deprived requires two fundamental conditions: a heart that has been softened by the remembrance of Allah and His signs, and knowledge and intellect ('aql) that is forward-looking and knows that after the downfalls, there are growths, "يُحْيِي الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا" (He revives the earth after its death), and the reduction of wealth today is the cause of its increase. "يُضَاعَفُ... أَجْرٌ كَرِيمٌ" (He will multiply... a generous reward).

Messages

1- To receive divine favors, both sadaqah (charity) and non-repayable aid are necessary, as is Qardh al-Hasanah (goodly loan) and aid that is returned. "الْمُصَّدِّقِينَ... أَقْرِضُوا اللَّهَ"

2- Lending to the servants of Allah is considered lending to Allah. "أَقْرِضُوا اللَّهَ" (Lend to Allah) instead of "أَقْرِضُوا النَّاسَ" (Lend to the people).

3- Men and women are equal in the right of ownership, solving economic problems, spiritual growth, and attaining otherworldly perfections. "إِنَّ الْمُصَّدِّقِينَ وَ الْمُصَّدِّقَاتِ"

4- What is important is saving the poor from poverty, whether through infaq (spending/charity) or through loans. "الْمُصَّدِّقِينَ... أَقْرِضُوا..."

5- Loans and infaq, outwardly, appear to be a reduction and removal of wealth, but in reality, they are growth. "يُضَاعَفُ"

6- Setting a reward is a way to encourage people toward good deeds. "يُضَاعَفُ... أَجْرٌ كَرِيمٌ"

7- Infaq and lending are signs of generosity (karam), and the reward of the Generous (Karim) is a generous reward (ajr karim). "الْمُصَّدِّقِينَ... أَقْرِضُوا... لَهُمْ أَجْرٌ كَرِيمٌ"

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِۦٓ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلصِّدِّيقُونَ ۖ وَٱلشُّهَدَآءُ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ لَهُمْ أَجْرُهُمْ وَنُورُهُمْ ۖ وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَكَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَآ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَحِيمِ (۱۹)
Those who have faith in Allah and His apostles—it is they who are the truthful and witnesses with their Lord; they shall have their reward and their light. But as for those who are faithless and deny Our signs, they shall be the inmates of hell. (19)

Key Points

"Siddīq" (the truthful one) is said of a person who is entirely truth and righteousness, whose heart, tongue, and action are unified, and for whom sincerity has become part of his character. In the Quran, this title has been given to individuals such as Abraham, Idris, Joseph, and Mary علیهم السلام, but this verse has opened the path to becoming a Siddīq for all those who possess faith. "Shuhadā’" (witnesses) means those who bear witness, which also includes martyrs (Shahīdān) on the battlefield.

Messages

1- Faith in Allah and all the Prophets is a sign of human sincerity (Sidq). «آمنوا باللّه ورسله... هم الصّدیقون»

2- For those who benefited from the light of the Prophets' guidance in this world, there will be light on the Day of Judgment. «لهم أجرهم و نورهم»

3- Encouraging the righteous and warning the wicked must occur side by side. «أولئك هم الصّدّیقون... أولئك أصحاب الجحیم»

4- Believers possess both reward (Ajr) and light (Nūr). «أجرهم و نورهم» But the disbelievers possess only punishment. «أصحاب الجحیم»

Ayah

ٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّمَا ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا لَعِبٌ وَلَهْوٌ وَزِينَةٌ وَتَفَاخُرٌۢ بَيْنَكُمْ وَتَكَاثُرٌ فِى ٱلْأَمْوَٰلِ وَٱلْأَوْلَـٰدِ ۖ كَمَثَلِ غَيْثٍ أَعْجَبَ ٱلْكُفَّارَ نَبَاتُهُۥ ثُمَّ يَهِيجُ فَتَرَىٰهُ مُصْفَرًّا ثُمَّ يَكُونُ حُطَـٰمًا ۖ وَفِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ عَذَابٌ شَدِيدٌ وَمَغْفِرَةٌ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَرِضْوَٰنٌ ۚ وَمَا ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَآ إِلَّا مَتَـٰعُ ٱلْغُرُورِ (۲٠)
Know that the life of this world is mere diversion and play, glamour and mutual vainglory among you and rivalry for wealth and children—like rain, whose growth impresses the farmer. Then it withers and you see it turn yellow, then it becomes chaff. Whereas in the Hereafter there is forgiveness from Allah and His approval and a severe punishment. The life of this world is nothing but the wares of delusion. (20)

Key Points

«Ghayth» (غَیث) derived from «Ghiyāth» (غیاث) means beneficial rain, whereas «Maṭar» (مطر) includes harmful rain as well. Perhaps it is called «Ghayth» because rain is the answer to the cries and needs of the people.

«Kuffār» (كُفّار) here means farmers, not disbelievers, because it originally derives from «Kufr» (كفر) meaning to cover. A farmer is called Kāfir or Kuffār because he sows the seed and covers it under the soil.

«Yahīj» (یهیج) derived from Hayajān (هیجان) has two meanings: the drying up of the plant, and excitement/agitation.

«Huṭām» (حطام) means a plant that breaks due to extreme dryness, like straw.

The description of the world (Dunya) as diversion (lahw) and play (la'ib) has appeared four times in the Qur'an, specifically in the form of exclusivity and restriction: 1. «و ما الحیاة الدّنیا الاّ لعب و لهو» (74) 2. «و ما هذه الحیاة الدّنیا الاّ لهو و لعب» (75) 3. «انّما الحیاة الدّنیا لعب و لهو» (76) 4. «اعلموا انّما الحیاة الدّنیا لعب و لهو» (77)

Perhaps by synthesizing the Quranic verses regarding the world, one can conclude that whenever we view the world as the ultimate goal and destination, it becomes a means of heedlessness and delusion. However, if we view the world as the farm for the Hereafter, a prelude and means to achieving perfection, then the world and its blessings will be valuable and constructive. Therefore, from this very worldly life, one group benefits in such a way that they receive Divine forgiveness (Maghfirah) and pleasure (Riḍwān), while another group acts in such a way that they become afflicted by severe punishment.

This verse refers to the different stages of human life (childhood and adolescence, youth, and adulthood) and explains that in every period of life, one of the manifestations of the world preoccupies man: The period of childhood and adolescence is preoccupied with diversion (lahw) and play (la'ib); the period of youth is concerned with adornment and beauty (zīnah); and the period of middle age and old age is afflicted by boasting (tafākhur) and rivalry in increase (takāthur).

In the previous verses, the discussion was about charity (ṣadaqah) and benevolent loans (qarḍ al-ḥasanah). This verse explains the obstacles and deterrents to spending (infāq).

74) Al-An‘ām, 32. 75) Al-‘Ankabūt, 64. 76) Muḥammad, 36. 77) Al-Ḥadīd, 20.

Messages

1- Recognizing the reality of the world requires precision and deep insight. «اعلموا» (Know).

2- The Qur'an corrects man's perspective regarding the world. «و اعلموا انما الحیاة الدنیا» (And know that the life of this world is only).

3- The beauties of the world are fleeting. «و زینة... تراه مصفرّاً» (and adornment... you see it withered).

4- The world always has spring and autumn, and whatever has the color of the world is subject to falling leaves. «أعجب الكفّار نباته ثمّ یهیج فتراه مصفرّا ثمّ یكون حطاماً» (Its growth pleases the farmers; then it withers, and you see it yellowed, then it becomes debris).

5- Play and diversion are characteristics of worldly life, but if they are not placed on the right path, they lead to the punishment of the Hereafter. «لعب و لهو... أعجب الكفّار... و فى الاخرة عذاب شدید» (play and diversion... pleases the farmers... and in the Hereafter is severe punishment).

6- Worldly adornments, such as personal jewelry, houses, and cars, if they lead to boasting and rivalry (tafākhur), drag man toward Hellfire. «زینة وتفاخر... فى الآخرة عذاب شدید» (adornment and boasting... in the Hereafter is severe punishment).

7- The abundance of children and population increase is valuable only if it does not lead to an abundance of Hell-dwellers, and if the children are raised with proper upbringing. «تكاثر فى... الاولاد... و فى الآخرة عذاب شدید» (rivalry in increase in... children... and in the Hereafter is severe punishment).

8- For some, the world causes deception and retribution, «فى الاخرة عذاب شدید» (in the Hereafter is severe punishment). For the believer, it is a means of growth, perfection, and attaining Divine forgiveness and pleasure. «انما الحیاة الدنیا... و مغفرة من اللّه و رضوان» (The life of this world is only... and forgiveness from Allah and pleasure).

9- Unless shortcomings are removed (through forgiveness), achieving Divine pleasure will be difficult. «مغفرة من اللّه و رضوان» (forgiveness from Allah and pleasure).

10- Severe punishment is the reaction to man's own deeds, but forgiveness and grace are from Allah. «مغفرة من اللّه» (forgiveness from Allah). (He attributed forgiveness and pleasure to Allah).

11- Man must remain between fear and hope. «عذاب شدید و مغفرة من اللّه و رضوان» (severe punishment and forgiveness from Allah and pleasure).

In education and training, three principles are necessary: explanation, analogy, and conclusion. «اعلموا» (Know) is for explanation, «كمثل غیث» (like the example of rain) is for analogy, and «عذاب شدید و مغفرة» (severe punishment and forgiveness) is the conclusion.

Ayah

سَابِقُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ مَغْفِرَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَجَنَّةٍ عَرْضُهَا كَعَرْضِ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ أُعِدَّتْ لِلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِۦ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ يُؤْتِيهِ مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ ذُو ٱلْفَضْلِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ (۲۱)
Take the lead towards forgiveness from your Lord and a paradise as vast as the heavens and the earth, prepared for those who have faith in Allah and His apostles. That is Allah’s grace, which He grants to whomever He wishes, and Allah is dispenser of a great grace. (21)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the discussion was that the result of worldly deeds is either severe punishment or forgiveness and pleasure (of Allah). In this Ayah, emphasis is placed on racing and surpassing one another in order to attain divine grace and mercy.

The Spiritual Race in the Quran: By reflecting briefly upon the Ayah, we realize the conditions and characteristics of the spiritual race, including: Participation is open to everyone. «سابقوا» The subject of the race is spiritual matters. «مغفرة» The Organizer is Allah. «سابقوا» Its prizes are determined beforehand. «اُعدّت» It has no specific time or place. «سابقوا» is absolute. The type of prize and its characteristics are clear. «جنّة عرضها كعرض السّماء» The prize is based on divine grace. «ذلك فضل اللّه» There is no limitation whatsoever regarding the prizes. «ذوالفضل العظیم»

Messages

1- When rejecting an undesirable matter, a suitable alternative must be provided. «متاع الغرور... سابقوا الى مغفرة...» Instead of racing toward boasting and accumulation (which was mentioned in the previous Ayah), race toward forgiveness and Paradise.

2- What gives value to good deeds is speed and precedence. «سابقوا...»

3- Forgiveness and pardon are among the attributes of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «مغفرة من ربّكم»

4- First being forgiven, and then entering Paradise. «مغفرة... و جنّة...»

5- Make the choice easier for people by increasing their knowledge and understanding of this world and the Hereafter. «فتراه مصفرّاً... ثم یكون حطاماً... جنة عرضها كعرض السّماء...»

6- Giving up a world whose provision is meager and deceptive leads you to a Paradise whose expanse is the expanse of the heavens and the earth. «كعرض السماء و الارض...»

7- Belief in all the Messengers is a prerequisite for belief in Allah. «آمنوا باللّه و رسله»

8- Paradise is the grace (فضل) of Allah bestowed upon the believers. «ذلك فضل اللّه»

9- Let us not judge whether individuals are destined for Paradise or Hell, for Allah deals with His grace (فضل) on the Day of Resurrection, and perhaps those whom we consider inhabitants of the Fire are inhabitants of Paradise in the sight of Allah. «یؤتیه من یشاء و اللّه ذو الفضل العظیم»

Ayah

مَآ أَصَابَ مِن مُّصِيبَةٍ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِىٓ أَنفُسِكُمْ إِلَّا فِى كِتَـٰبٍ مِّن قَبْلِ أَن نَّبْرَأَهَآ ۚ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ يَسِيرٌ (۲۲)
No affliction visits the land or yourselves but it is in a Book before We bring it about—that is indeed easy for Allah— (22)

Key Points

Nabra'ahā (نبرأها) is derived from bar' (برء), meaning to bring into existence. Allah, Who brought the earth into existence and created humanity, has also decreed difficulties and calamities for them, such as earthquakes, famine, and floods for the earth, and illness, injury, and death for humans. It is mentioned in historical texts that Yazid said to the family of Imam Husayn عليه السلام, who were captives in his hands: The calamity that befell you was due to your own actions, and he recited Ayah 30 of Surah Ash-Shura: «ما اصابكم من مصیبة فبما كسبت ایدیكم...». Imam Sajjad عليه السلام replied: This Ayah was not revealed concerning us, and then He recited the Ayah under discussion (Surah Al-Hadid 57:22). Meaning, Allah had predetermined that we should endure such hardships and difficulties for the preservation of His religion. We read in a Hadith: Calamities are a means of discipline and warning for the oppressor, but for the believers, they are a means of trial and growth, and for the prophets, a means of attaining ranks, and for the friends of Allah, a means of receiving honor. «البلاء للظالم أدب و للمؤمن امتحان و للانبیاء درجة و للاولیاء كرامة»(78)

78) Tafsir Namuneh and Bihar, Vol. 64, p. 235.

Messages

1- Natural occurrences such as floods and earthquakes, which cause the destruction of homes and farms («مصیبة فى الارض» - a calamity in the earth), or injuries and deaths («فى أنفسكم» - in yourselves), are based on a divinely pre-established system, and are not random events outside the power of Allah. «الاّ فى كتاب من قبل ان نبرأها»

2- Existence (the universe) operates according to a system and calculation, and all phenomena are recorded before they occur. «ما أصاب... الاّ فى كتاب»

3- No event is new or surprising to Allah. «فى كتاب من قبل ان نبرأها»

4- Do not be surprised by Allah's comprehensive knowledge of all natural events. «انّ ذلك على اللّه یسیر»

Ayah

لِّكَيْلَا تَأْسَوْا۟ عَلَىٰ مَا فَاتَكُمْ وَلَا تَفْرَحُوا۟ بِمَآ ءَاتَىٰكُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ كُلَّ مُخْتَالٍ فَخُورٍ (۲۳)
so that you may not grieve for what escapes you, nor boast for what comes your way, and Allah does not like any swaggering braggart. (23)

Key Points

Mukhṭāl (مختال) means arrogance based on the illusion and imagination of greatness. This Ayah is the reason for the previous Ayah. That is, the reason We informed you that We have decreed and written down events before they occur is so that you do not grieve over a blessing you lose, nor rejoice over a blessing Allah grants you, and know that blessings are a divine trust entrusted to you. This does not mean we should turn our backs on divine gifts and not benefit from them, but rather that we should not become enslaved by them or consider them our ultimate goal and destination. (79) All bitter and sweet events are like steps made of black and white stones, meant for ascending and reaching perfection, and one must quickly pass over them without paying attention to the color of the steps. A bank employee is responsible for receiving money from people one day and paying it out the next. He neither loses his composure on the first day nor rejoices on the second, because he knows he is merely a trustworthy employee. By decreeing events and ups and downs, Allah intends to elevate the human spirit so much that bitterness and sweetness do not affect it, just as Lady Zaynab عليه السلام said after the events of Karbala: "I saw nothing but beauty," «و ما رأیتُ الا جمیلا» (80) And Imam Husayn عليه السلام, in the pit of the battlefield and in the last moments of His life, said: "O God! I am content with Your decree, and patient regarding Your trial and test," «رضاً بقضائك، صبراً على بلائك» And Imam Ali عليه السلام considered His longing and familiarity with martyrdom greater than the familiarity of a child with his mother's breast. «و اللّه لابن ابى طالب آنس بالموت من الطفل بثدى امّه» (81) If we know that events are based on Allah's knowledge and wisdom, not accidental or without reason, and if we know the wisdom behind the occurrence of events, we will not envy, we will not be greedy, we will not harbor enmity, and we will not become angry, because we know that the scenes have been wisely designed beforehand. If we know that the granting of a blessing brings responsibility and duty, we will not rejoice in possessing it, and if we know that the removal of a blessing will be compensated for on the Day of Judgment, we will not grieve over what we have lost. Imam Ali عليه السلام said: All asceticism (zuhd) is contained in two words of the Quran. He then recited this Ayah and said: Do not regret what you have lost, and do not be delighted by what is given to you. Whoever is like this is an ascetic (zāhid) comprehensive in all aspects. (82)

79) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 80) Luhūf, p. 160 81) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 5. 82) Nahj al-Balaghah, Maxim 439.

Messages

1- Events have a purpose. «ما أصاب من مصیبة... لكیلا تأسوا على ما فاتكم...»

2- Blessings are from Allah, «آتاكم» (He gave you), but the removal of a blessing is not attributed to Allah. «ما فاتكم» (what you missed/lost).

3- Knowledge of the effects and goals of events leads to tranquility. «فى كتاب من قبل ان نبرءها... لكیلا تأسوا»

4- Allah informed [us] about the recording of events in the Preserved Tablet (Lawḥ Maḥfūẓ) so that servants do not grieve or become arrogant. «لكیلا تأسوا... و لاتفرحوا»

5- Natural sadness and joy are not bad; what is condemned is excessive grieving over the past or becoming intoxicated by possessions. «لكیلا تأسوا... و لاتفرحوا»

6- Whoever is heedless of divine decrees assumes he possesses a virtue for which Allah granted him the blessing, and thus boasts over others. «مختال فخور»

7- Inner illusions are the prelude to external boasting. «مختال فخور»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يَبْخَلُونَ وَيَأْمُرُونَ ٱلنَّاسَ بِٱلْبُخْلِ ۗ وَمَن يَتَوَلَّ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلْغَنِىُّ ٱلْحَمِيدُ (۲٤)
Such as are [themselves] stingy and bid [other] people to be stingy. And whoever refuses to comply [should know that] indeed Allah is the All-sufficient, the All-laudable. (24)

Key Points

The root of stinginess is the preservation of superiority and boasting, because if blessings are also in the hands of others, the means of boasting are lost. Imam Ali عليه السلام in Sermon 183 of Nahj al-Balagha says: "Fear Allah, fear Allah, O assembly of servants! While you are safe in health before sickness, and while you are in ease before hardship overtakes you. Strive then to free your necks before their pledges are locked up. Keep your eyes awake (in worship), keep your stomachs empty (by fasting), use your feet (for good deeds), spend your wealth, take from your physical bodies and be generous with it towards your souls, and do not be stingy towards them. For Allah, the Glorified, has said: «ان تنصروا اللّه ینصركم و یثبّت اقدامكم» (If you support Allah, He will support you and make your steps firm.) And He, the Exalted, said: «من ذا الّذى یقرض اللّه قرضاً حسنا فیضاعفه له و له اجر كریم» (Who is it that would loan Allah a goodly loan so He may multiply it for him and he will have a noble reward?) He did not seek your help out of humiliation, nor did He seek a loan from you out of need. He sought your help while the armies of the heavens and the earth belong to Him, and He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise. He sought a loan from you while the treasures of the heavens and the earth belong to Him, and He is the Self-Sufficient, the Praiseworthy. He only intended to test you to see which of you is best in deed. So hasten with your deeds so that you may be with the neighbors of Allah in His abode."

Portrait of Stinginess in Narrations Imam Ali عليه السلام asked his son Imam Hassan عليه السلام: What is shuhh (extreme avarice)? He replied: «ان ترى ما فى یدك شرفاً و ما انفقت تلفا» (83) "That you consider what is in your hand an honor, and what you spend a waste." «البخل بالموجود سوء الظّن بالمعبود» (84) Stinginess with what is available is ill-opinion of the Worshipped (Allah). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was praying one night while performing Tawaf (circumambulation), saying: «اللّهم قِنى شحّ نفسى» (85) "O Allah! Protect me from the avarice of my soul." Stinginess is not only related to wealth; rather, some people are stingy even regarding respect for others. «البخیل من بخل بالسلام» (86) The stingy person is he who is stingy with the greeting of peace (Salam). «البخل باخراج ما افترض اللّه من الاموال اقبح البخل» (87) Stinginess in paying what Allah has made obligatory from wealth is the ugliest stinginess. The Prophet said: «البخیل حقاً مَن ذُكرت عنده فلم یُصلّ علىَّ» (88) The truly stingy person is he before whom my name is mentioned, but he does not send blessings (Salawat) upon me. «لاتدخلن فى مشورتك بخیلا» (89) Do not include a stingy person in your consultation. «النظر الى البخیل یقسى القلب» (90) Looking at the stingy person hardens the heart.

83) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 305. 84) Ghurar al-Hikam. 85) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 70, p. 301. 86) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 70, p. 305. 87) Ghurar al-Hikam. 88) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 306. 89) Nahj al-Balagha, Letter 53. 90) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 78, p. 53.

Messages

  1. Being stingy and commanding others to be stingy are signs of arrogant and boastful individuals. «مختال فخور الّذین یبخلون و یأمرون النّاس بالبخل»
  2. The stingy person is not beloved by Allah. «و اللّه لایحبّ كلّ مختال فخور الّذین یبخلون»
  3. Sin becomes dangerous when it becomes a person's habit or way of life. «یبخلون و یأمرون... بالبخل»
  4. Because the stingy person knows that if others are generous, his stinginess will become apparent and cause his disgrace, he commands others to be stingy. «یأمرون الناس بالبخل»
  5. Stinginess is aversion and turning away from the command of Allah. «و من یتولّ»
  6. Being Self-Sufficient (Ghaniyy) is not enough; being Praiseworthy (Hamid) and beloved by people is also necessary, which is achieved through spending (in charity) and lending. (Usually, stingy people are wealthy but are not praised by people because of their stinginess. Therefore, this verse says about Allah:) «فانّ اللّه هو الغنىّ الحمید»

Ayah

لَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا رُسُلَنَا بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ وَأَنزَلْنَا مَعَهُمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَٱلْمِيزَانَ لِيَقُومَ ٱلنَّاسُ بِٱلْقِسْطِ ۖ وَأَنزَلْنَا ٱلْحَدِيدَ فِيهِ بَأْسٌ شَدِيدٌ وَمَنَـٰفِعُ لِلنَّاسِ وَلِيَعْلَمَ ٱللَّهُ مَن يَنصُرُهُۥ وَرُسُلَهُۥ بِٱلْغَيْبِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ قَوِىٌّ عَزِيزٌ (۲٥)
Certainly We sent Our apostles with manifest proofs, and We sent down with them the Book and the Balance, so that mankind may maintain justice; and We sent down iron, in which there is great might and uses for mankind, and so that Allah may know those who help Him and His apostles [with faith] in the Unseen. Indeed Allah is all-strong, all-mighty. (25)

Key Points

The meaning of al-Bayyināt (the clear proofs) is the illuminating miracles that complete the argument (proof) against the people. The meaning of al-Kitāb (the Book) is the heavenly revelation that must be written down and become a book. The meaning of al-Mīzān (the Balance) is either the scale used in transactions, which is a symbol of equity and justice, or the laws of the Shari'ah, which are the criterion for distinguishing truth from falsehood, or human intellect, which is the measure for recognizing good from evil. (91) The meaning of the sending down (inzāl) of iron in anzalnā al-Ḥadīd (We sent down the iron) is similar to the sending down of livestock in anzala lakum min al-an‘ām thamāniyata azwāj (He sent down for you of the cattle eight pairs). (92) This is because, based on the Ayah wa in min shay’in illā ‘indanā khazā’inuhu wa mā nunazziluhu illā bi-qadarin ma‘lūm (And there is not a thing but that with Us are its treasures, and We do not send it down except in a known measure) (93), the source of existence for everything is with Allah, and He brings every being into this world after determining its existential limits, which is itself a type of descent (nuzūl). (94) The meaning of yanṣuruhu... bi-l-ghayb (helps Him... unseen) is that they help the unseen God and His Messengers without having seen them. It is clear that the meaning of helping God is helping His religion, which has been conveyed to the people by the Messengers. The utilization of iron has been mentioned many times in the Quran. Dhul-Qarnayn requested iron from the people to build his famous barrier. The softening of iron in the hands of Prophet Dawud (David) was one of the special favors Allah granted him: alannā lahu al-ḥadīd (We made the iron soft for him). (95) In this Ayah (Surah Al-Hadid), the word manāfi‘ (benefits) is used for iron. In the Hereafter, some tools for punishing criminals will also be made of iron: wa lahum maqāmi‘u min ḥadīd (And for them are maces of iron). (96)

91) Tafsir al-Mizan. 92) Az-Zumar, 6. 93) Al-Hijr, 21. 94) Tafsir al-Mizan. 95) Saba’, 10. 96) Al-Hajj, 22.

Messages

1- In management, whoever we give responsibility to, we must provide them with the tools for the job. arsalnā rusulanā bi-l-bayyināt wa... al-kitāb wa al-mīzān.

2- In sound management, guidance comes from the manager, and action comes from the people. arsalnā... al-kitāb wa al-mīzān... li-yaqūma an-nāsu bi-l-qisṭ.

3- True justice is achieved under the shadow of divine law and a divine leader. rusulanā... al-kitāb... li-yaqūma an-nāsu bi-l-qisṭ.

4- The philosophy of prophethood is the establishment of justice. arsalnā rusulanā... li-yaqūma an-nāsu bi-l-qisṭ.

5- After the completion of the proof (ḥujjah) by Allah, the responsibility lies with the people to rise up for action and implementation. arsalnā... anzalnā... li-yaqūma an-nāsu bi-l-qisṭ.

6- Justice is desirable in all dimensions. bi-l-qisṭ (with justice) is mentioned absolutely, encompassing everything. li-yaqūma an-nāsu bi-l-qisṭ.

7- All Prophets share a single goal. arsalnā rusulanā... li-yaqūma an-nāsu bi-l-qisṭ.

8- People must have a government and organization so they can rise up for justice and stop the transgressors. li-yaqūma an-nāsu bi-l-qisṭ.

9- First, cultural and educational work, then the application of power and punishment. anzalnā... al-kitāb wa al-mīzān... wa anzalnā al-ḥadīd.

10- Society requires three powers: legislative (al-Kitāb), judicial (al-Mīzān li-yaqūma an-nāsu bi-l-qisṭ), and executive. anzalnā al-ḥadīd fīhi ba’sun shadīd.

11- Obstacles to the spread of justice must be dealt with severely. li-yaqūma an-nāsu bi-l-qisṭ wa anzalnā al-ḥadīd fīhi ba’sun shadīd.

12- Accepting the invitation of the Prophets is, in reality, helping Allah. wa li-ya‘lama Allāhu man yanṣuruhu.

13- Support for the religion and the friends (awliyā’) of Allah is valuable only when it comes from the depths of the heart and with willingness of the soul. man yanṣuruhu wa rusulahu bi-l-ghayb.

14- Let us not imagine that Allah is in need of our help. li-ya‘lama Allāhu man yanṣuruhu... inna Allāha qawiyyun ‘azīz.

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا وَإِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَجَعَلْنَا فِى ذُرِّيَّتِهِمَا ٱلنُّبُوَّةَ وَٱلْكِتَـٰبَ ۖ فَمِنْهُم مُّهْتَدٍ ۖ وَكَثِيرٌ مِّنْهُمْ فَـٰسِقُونَ (۲٦)
Certainly We sent Noah and Abraham and We ordained among their descendants prophethood and the Book. Some of them are [rightly] guided, and many of them are transgressors. (26)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the discussion was about sending prophets in general. In this Ayah, He mentions two examples of role models who are the progenitors of the prophets who came after them. Allah also placed the Imamate in the lineage and progeny of the Prophet of Islam. This does not mean that the system of prophethood (Nubuwwah) or Imamate is like a hereditary monarchy. Rather, it means that inner purity (ṭahārat bāṭin), necessary ability, and talent are transferred to the children and descendants, and if the other conditions are met, that person attains the station of prophethood or Imamate. Therefore, when Hazrat Ibrahim (Abraham) requested the station of Imamate for his children and said: «و من ذرّیتى» (and from my progeny), Allah replied: «لا ینال عهدى الظالمین»(97) (My covenant does not reach the wrongdoers). My covenant of Imamate does not reach the unworthy among your descendants. Thus, the main criterion for the appointment of a prophet or Imam is worthiness and competence, not inheritance.

97) Baqarah, 124.

Messages

1- When mentioning great figures, prioritize the pioneers. «نوحاً و ابراهیم»

2- The station of prophethood is by Divine appointment, not popular election. «جعلنا فى ذرّیتهما النّبوّة»

3- Acceptance or rejection of the prophets' call has always existed. «فمنهم مهتد و كثیر...»

4- In matters of belief and morality, the majority is not a sign of truthfulness. «كثیر منهم فاسقون»

5- Being from the lineage and progeny of the pure is not proof of purity. «كثیر منهم فاسقون»

Ayah

ثُمَّ قَفَّيْنَا عَلَىٰٓ ءَاثَـٰرِهِم بِرُسُلِنَا وَقَفَّيْنَا بِعِيسَى ٱبْنِ مَرْيَمَ وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ ٱلْإِنجِيلَ وَجَعَلْنَا فِى قُلُوبِ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّبَعُوهُ رَأْفَةً وَرَحْمَةً وَرَهْبَانِيَّةً ٱبْتَدَعُوهَا مَا كَتَبْنَـٰهَا عَلَيْهِمْ إِلَّا ٱبْتِغَآءَ رِضْوَٰنِ ٱللَّهِ فَمَا رَعَوْهَا حَقَّ رِعَايَتِهَا ۖ فَـَٔاتَيْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مِنْهُمْ أَجْرَهُمْ ۖ وَكَثِيرٌ مِّنْهُمْ فَـٰسِقُونَ (۲۷)
Then We followed them up with Our apostles and We followed [them] with Jesus son of Mary, and We gave him the Evangel, and We put kindness and mercy into the hearts of those who followed him. But as for monasticism, they innovated it—We had not prescribed it for them—only seeking Allah’s pleasure. Yet they did not observe it with due observance. So We gave to the faithful among them their [due] reward, but many of them are transgressors. (27)

Key Points

«رهبانیت» (Rahbaniyyah/Monasticism) comes from rahbah, meaning fear and awe (khashyah). Those who withdraw from people and dedicate themselves to the worship of God out of awe of Allah are called Ruhban (monks). Although Allah had not made Rahbaniyyah obligatory upon the followers of Christ عليه السلام, and they themselves had made it necessary upon themselves seeking Allah's pleasure, they did not fulfill its right and did not observe its limits. This is similar to supererogatory prayer and fasting which, if a person makes obligatory upon himself through a vow, he must complete. It is possible that the meaning of the Ayah is that the companions of Christ went after Rahbaniyyah on their own initiative, and We did not make it obligatory upon them, but if they had observed its limits, it would have been pleasing to Us. Just as the Companions of the Cave withdrew from people and became cave-dwellers to preserve their religion.

Bid'ah (innovation) means novelty. In scientific and industrial matters, invention and innovation are commendable things, but in religious matters, the foundation of which is Divine laws, introducing any personal belief or preference is considered Bid'ah and is unacceptable.

Allah desires complete action, thus He censures those who do not fulfill the right of an action, saying: «فما رعوها حقّ رعایتها» (they did not observe it with the right observance). And in other instances, He has commanded the believers to observe the right of piety and struggle: «اتّقوا اللّه حق تقاته»(98) and «...حقّ جهاده»(99).

Rahbaniyyah in Islam

The Holy Quran has rejected Rahbaniyyah and the abandonment of the world in several verses, such as: «یا أیّها النّبىّ لِمَ تحرّم ما احلّ اللّه لك»(100) O Prophet! Why do you forbid what Allah has made lawful for you? «قل مَن حرّم زینة اللّه التى اخرج لعباده و الطیبات من الرزق»(101) Say: Who has forbidden the adornment of Allah which He has produced for His servants and the good things of provision? «یا أیّها الّذین آمنوا لاتحرّموا طیّبات ما احلّ اللّه لكم»(102) O believers, do not forbid the good things which Allah has made lawful for you.

It is recorded in history that a group of Muslims swore not to have relations with their wives, to fast perpetually, and to stay awake at night. The Prophet summoned them all to the mosque and said: I marry and I sleep at night; whoever does not follow my Sunnah is not of me.(103)

When the son of Uthman ibn Maz'un passed away, his father became isolated in his house. The Prophet summoned him and said: «لم یكتب علینا الرهبانیّة انّما رهبانیّة أمّتى الجهاد فى سبیل اللّه»(104) Allah has not prescribed Rahbaniyyah upon us. The Rahbaniyyah of my Ummah is Jihad in the way of Allah.

Imam Ali عليه السلام entered the house of Alaa ibn Ziyad to visit him. He had a spacious house. The Imam told him: You are more in need of this house in the Hereafter; you must use this large house for maintaining kinship ties, hosting guests, etc., so that you may gain the Hereafter through it. Alaa said: But my brother Asim has abandoned the world. The Imam summoned him and said: «یا عدىّ نفسه لقد استهام بك الخبیث اما رحمت اهلك و وُلدك اترى اللّه احلّ لك الطیّبات و هو یكره ان تأخذها» O enemy of the self, the wicked one (Satan) has led you astray. Why do you not have mercy on your family and children? Do you not see that Allah has made the good things lawful for you and dislikes that you abandon them? Asim said: Then why are your food and clothing like this? The Imam عليه السلام said: «انّى لست كأنتَ اَنّ اللّه تعالى فرض على ائمّة الحق أن یقدّروا انفسهم بضعفة الناس»(105) My case is separate from yours. Allah the Almighty has obligated the Imams of Truth to measure themselves by the weak among the people.

Of course, the conditions of every time are also different. Sometimes the environment of life and the culture of the people are such that they must be observed, just as it is recommended not to laugh during a funeral procession, or the Imam commanded that if wheat is scarce, our food should not be made of wheat, or it is recommended that if corruption is prevalent in society, having good faith is not the correct action. In any case, the conditions of the audience and the time are different, and thus we read the following in history regarding Imam Reza عليه السلام: A group of Sufis told Imam Reza عليه السلام: Your clothing is fine, but an Imam's clothing, food, and mount should be simple. The Imam said: Joseph was a Prophet of God, yet he wore silk brocade and sat upon a throne. Indeed, what is expected of an Imam is equity and justice. Then He recited this Ayah: «قل من حرّم زینة اللّه...».(106)

Sometimes the Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله would rebuke some of His wives who were disheveled, saying: «مالى اراك شعثاء مرهاء سلتاء»(107) Why do I see you (in the house) with disheveled, untidy, and unadorned hair?

From the fact that the word Injil (Gospel) is singular, we understand that only one Gospel was revealed to Christ عليه السلام. So where did the multiple Gospels of today come from?

The sign of the followers of Christ عليه السلام is tenderness and mercy «اتّبعوه رأفة و رحمة», but the sign of the companions of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله is mercy towards their own and severity towards the adversaries. «و الّذین معه اشداء على الكفار رحماء بینهم»(108)

The words «رئوف» (Ra’uf/Tender) and «رحیم» (Rahim/Merciful) have appeared together ten times in the Quran. Ra’uf means compassion for the removal of hardship and bitterness, and Rahim means affection and mercy after the removal of bitterness and hardship. Ra’fah concerns the repulsion of evil, and Rahmah concerns the attraction of good.

98) Aal-i Imran, 102. 99) Hajj, 78. 100) Tahrim, 1. 101) A'raf, 32. 102) Ma'idah, 87. 103) Bihar, Vol. 67, p. 117. 104) Bihar, Vol. 67, p. 115. 105) Bihar, Vol. 67, p. 118. 106) Bihar, Vol. 67, p. 121. 107) Bihar, Vol. 67, p. 122. 108) Fath, 29.

Messages

1- Throughout history, Prophets have come successively, and the flow of prophethood has been a continuous matter. «قفّینا على آثارهم... قفّینا»

2- Special individuals must be specially honored; mentioning Noah, Abraham, and Jesus علیهم السلام alongside the general title of «رسلنا, رسله» (Our Messengers, His Messengers) is a sign of special honor. «قفّینا... برسلنا و قفّینا بعیسى بن مریم»

3- The good qualities and characteristics of the followers of every religion and school of thought must be mentioned. «جعلنا فى قلوب الّذین اتّبعوه رأفةً و رحمةً»

4- Following the Prophets is the basis for receiving purity of heart, tenderness, and mercy. «اتبعوه رأفةً و رحمةً»

5- One must both remove the obstacles to intimacy (Ra’fah) and create the factors of affection (Rahmah).

6- Subjective innovations and Bid'ah in religion are not acceptable to Allah. «رهبانیّة ابتدعوها ما كتبناها علیهم»

7- Bid'ah is one of the afflictions and harms of Divine religions. «رهبانیّة ابتدعوها»

8- How often pure and sincere motives, because they were not in line with the Divine Law, led to superstitions and Bid'ah. «رهبانیّة ابتدعوها... ابتغاء رضوان اللّه»

9- Disbelief (Kufr) and transgression (Fisq) are companions. (In contrast to faith, transgression is mentioned, whereas disbelief should have been raised.) «الّذین آمنوا... كثیر منهم فاسقون»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَءَامِنُوا۟ بِرَسُولِهِۦ يُؤْتِكُمْ كِفْلَيْنِ مِن رَّحْمَتِهِۦ وَيَجْعَل لَّكُمْ نُورًا تَمْشُونَ بِهِۦ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۲۸)
O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah and have faith in His Apostle. He will grant you a double share of His mercy and a light to walk by, and He will forgive you, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful; (28)

Key Points

The addressees of this Ayah could be the Christians mentioned in the previous Ayah, so that they fulfill the right of abandoning the world (asceticism/monasticism) and, after (the time of) Jesus عليه السلام, believe in the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله. Thus, they would have both the reward for believing in Christ عليه السلام and the recompense for believing in Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله. In a Hadith from the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, we read: Those Christians who believed in me fulfilled the right of monasticism. (109) Of course, the Ayah can be interpreted independently of the previous Ayah, meaning that Muslims should deepen their faith in Allah and the Messenger. It is possible that the intent of «كفلین من رحمته» (a double portion of His mercy) is one being inner light and the other being Divine forgiveness, which is mentioned later in the Ayah.

109) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan.

Messages

1- The prerequisite for believing in Allah is piety and God-fearingness (Taqwa). «یا أیّها الّذین آمنوا اتّقوا اللّه»

2- We must always deepen our faith. «آمَنوا... آمِنوا»

3- Both faith and piety (Taqwa) are a portion of mercy. «آمَنوا اتّقوا... كفلین من رحمته»

4- The pious believer is never trapped in a dead end. «آمنوا اتّقوا... یجعل لكم نوراً...»

5- The believer is not infallible (immune from sin), but he is pious (Muttaqi) and enters the field with the shield of Taqwa. Therefore, Allah forgives his errors. «اتّقوا اللّه... یغفر لكم و اللّه غفور رحیم»

6- In receiving Divine favors, age, race, tribe, or mere claims are not the criterion; faith and piety (Taqwa) are the criterion. «اتّقوا اللّه و آمنوا... یؤتكم كفلین من رحمته»

Ayah

لِّئَلَّا يَعْلَمَ أَهْلُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ أَلَّا يَقْدِرُونَ عَلَىٰ شَىْءٍ مِّن فَضْلِ ٱللَّهِ ۙ وَأَنَّ ٱلْفَضْلَ بِيَدِ ٱللَّهِ يُؤْتِيهِ مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ ذُو ٱلْفَضْلِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ (۲۹)
so that the People of the Book may know that they do not control Allah grace in any wise and that in Allah’s hand is all grace, which He grants to whomever He wishes and Allah is dispenser of a mighty grace. (29)

Key Points

This Surah began with the glorification (Tasbih) of Allah and ended with Divine grace (Fadl). In Ayah 54 of Surah Al-Qasas, Allah promises a double reward concerning the People of the Book who embrace Islam: «أجرهم مرتین» (their reward twice). In these verses, He also tells the Muslims: If you are people of piety (Taqwa) and faith in the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, your reward will also be double, so that the People of the Book do not imagine that Muslims are incapable of receiving Divine favors and that these favors are exclusive to them. Paying attention to the verses where the term «فضل عظیم» (great grace/favor) is used, it becomes clear that the great Divine grace is guidance, Paradise, forgiveness, prophethood, following the pleasure of Allah, and possessing correct insight and discernment. This expression has not been used regarding material blessings.

Messages

1- Explicitly prevent delusions, expectations, and deviations. «لئلا یعلم أهل الكتاب»

2- Divine bestowal depends on His wise Will, not on our imaginations. «انّ الفضل بید اللّه»

3- The Wise God chooses specific individuals to receive His grace. «یؤتیه من یشاء»
